threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it the Tube instead of just the Underground, and your face when you hear it's on strike is priceless.
Caught a Brixton night bus last week; two guys arguing loudly about which chicken shop is the best. Didn't realise it was such a fierce debate.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was really living in Neukölln when I saw a couple arguing passionately in Turkish over kebab toppings at 3 AM. The street life here never sleeps.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a kebab shop open at 4 AM that serves beer on tap. It's a late-night lifesaver after a long night out.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they know not to even think about catching the 14 bus at rush hour—it's a gamble you won't win.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Just saw a guy in a tuxedo hop on the 47, adjusting his bow tie. His look didn’t quite match the weathered seats, but hey, it’s SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the plazas, but the real charm is in the tiny streets where you find the best bakeries and hidden bars.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newcomer by their confused face when the tram doesn't stop at every stop, and they nervously check the map every two minutes.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look shocked when a bus arrives late and you didn’t see anyone pull a ‘told you so’ face. It's all part of the charm.
Caught a late 333 last night; two lads were discussing a hidden rooftop bar near Coldharbour. Heard it's got a killer view of the skyline.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a group of kids building a mini skatepark out of old pallets in the Liselotte-Herrmann-Straße. That's pure Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin will you find a kebab shop open at 3 AM serving fresh döners while artists set up their galleries on the sidewalk. Pure Kreuzberg vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they navigate BART delays without a second thought, while newcomers are still cursing their fate at the station.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy with a surfboard on Muni, he casually asked the driver if he could ride in front. Only in SF do you have to negotiate surf etiquette on public transit.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí, but the real charm is in the little squares where locals sip café amb llet, hidden from the tourist rush.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newcomer by their bewilderment at the lack of late-night transport - they’re still checking apps for buses at 2 AM.
